steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
For
$$x^2+9x+11=0$$
the coefficients are $a=1$, $b=9$, and $c=11$. The quadratic formula gives
$$x=\frac{-9\pm\sqrt{9^2-4(1)(11)}}{2(1)}$$

steps.title: Simplify the radical
steps.content:
Simplify the expression under the square root:
$$9^2-4(1)(11)=81-44=37$$
So one solution is
$$x=\frac{-9+\sqrt{37}}{2}$$
The answer is 37.
